Operation 'listConnectionHistory' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is generic and description is completely missing. Added specificity about pagination, filtering capabilities, return type (HistoryListResponse), and use cases. Included domain context about what connection history represents in the remote desktop service context. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.summary_suggestion: List connection history with filtering and pagination
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of connection history records for the authenticated user. Supports filtering by date range, agent, and connection type. Returns a HistoryListResponse containing session records with connection metadata including timestamps, participants, duration, and connection status. Useful for auditing remote access sessions and tracking support interactions.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic and description is completely missing. Added specificity about pagination, filtering capabilities, return type (HistoryListResponse), and use cases. Included domain context about what connection history represents in the remote desktop service context. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'getSessionInfo' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is empty and summary lacks specificity. Added details about required parameters (session ID), return type (SessionDetail), and practical use cases. data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve detailed information for a specific session
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific connection session by session ID. Returns a SessionDetail object containing connection metadata, participant information, duration, timestamps, and connection quality metrics. Requires the session ID as a path parameter. Use this operation to audit individual sessions, retrieve connection logs, or verify session information for support purposes.

data.description_suggestion: Logs out and detaches a permanent access agent from the authenticated account. Requires the agent ID in the request body. Returns a StatusResponse confirming the logout. data.description_suggestion: Consolidates multiple permanent access agents into a single agent entry. Requires specifying the source agents to merge and the target agent to merge into. The operation combines agent configurations and connection history. After merging, source agents become inactive and all future connections route through the target agent. Returns the updated target agent with consolidated information.

data.current_summary: Create a new quick support invitation
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new quick support invitation for temporary remote access. Accepts configuration parameters such as expiration duration, access restrictions, and optional description. Returns a SupportInvitation object containing the unique invitation ID, connection URL, and access details. The invitation becomes immediately active and can be shared with support personnel. Invitations automatically expire after the specified duration or can be manually closed.

data.description_suggestion: Updates properties of an existing quick support invitation. Accepts the invitation ID and fields to modify such as expiration time, access restrictions, or description. data.current_summary: Generate connection link for an invitation
data.description_suggestion: Generates a connection URL for establishing a remote desktop session through a quick support invitation. Requires the invitation ID as input. Returns a ConnectionUrlResponse containing the unique connection link that can be shared with the support agent. The connection link is valid for the invitation's remaining lifetime. Multiple connection links can be generated for the same invitation. Each connection is tracked in the session history.

data.description_suggestion: Closes an active quick support invitation, immediately preventing new connections through that invitation. Requires the invitation ID as input. Returns a StatusResponse confirming the closure. Existing active sessions through this invitation are not terminated, but no new connections can be established. Closed invitations cannot be reopened and must be recreated if needed. This operation is idempotent - closing an already-closed invitation returns success.

data.current_summary: Create a new quick support invitation
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new quick support invitation for temporary remote access. Accepts configuration parameters such as expiration duration, access restrictions, and optional description. Returns a SupportInvitation object containing the unique invitation ID, connection URL, and access details. The invitation becomes immediately active and can be shared with support personnel. Invitations automatically expire after the specified duration or can be manually closed.

data.current_summary: Generate connection link for an invitation
data.description_suggestion: Generates a connection URL for establishing a remote desktop session through a quick support invitation. Requires the invitation ID as input. Returns a ConnectionUrlResponse containing the unique connection link that can be shared with the support agent. The connection link is valid for the invitation's remaining lifetime. Multiple connection links can be generated for the same invitation. Each connection is tracked in the session history.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. data.description_suggestion: Closes an active quick support invitation, immediately preventing new connections through that invitation. Requires the invitation ID as input. Returns a StatusResponse confirming the closure. Existing active sessions through this invitation are not terminated, but no new connections can be established. Closed invitations cannot be reopened and must be recreated if needed. This operation is idempotent - closing an already-closed invitation returns success.
